strDesc: [us] [flicks] просматривает местные соцсети на [us-pos] PDA.

aCustomInfos: PDANotesAdd=NEW TO-DOs! - [x] Eat Breakfast - [x] Organise To-Dos - [ ] Buy new salvage license - [ ] Make enough to get through the day!

strDesc: [us] [sobs] тихо, обращаясь к [us-reflexive].
Ты делаешь паузу прежде чем, написать несколько длинных строчек домой используя свой КПК.

Что-то внутри корпуса жужжит, потом скрежещет, потом шипит. [them] [sighs]. Чертов [us] снова сломался.
[us] [hits] [them] чтобы все снова заработало.
[us] дрожит и брызгает слюной. Он все еще сломан.
[us] [flips] открывает [them-pos] корпус и [sets] приступает к его ремонту.
[us] [starts] снова работать, и [them] [places] свою канистру в слот в самый последний момент.
Что-то внутри [us] с жалостным хрустом ломается, давая понять, что [us] теперь разбиты без возможности немедленного восстановления.
[us] [throws] руки [us-pos].  Кофе не будет. Всем лучше не мешать [us-pos] way.

[us] [defies] [them].
[us] [throws] [us-pos] руки и сдается.
[us] [steps] в сторону [them] угрожающе.

[us] [asks] [them] как дела.

[us] [snubs] [them], и уходит.
[us] [shrugs] и [says] не много.
[us] [offers] [them] сигарету.
[us] [grows] немного возбужденным и [stamps] [us-pos] окурком по земле.

[us] [accepts] сигарету и [them-pos] предлагает огонь.

[us] и [them] наслаждаемся курением в комфортной тишине.
[us] и [them] наслаждаемся курением в атмосфере дружеского общения.
[us] и [them] наслаждаемся близостью, чувственными взглядами, общим дыханием и двусмысленными намеками, пока курим.
[us-pos] разговор замирает и заканчивается тем, что [us] и [them] молчаливо размышляют, покуривая сигареты.
[us] [waves] отказывается от предложенных сигарет Viceroy. Это не [us-pos] марка.
[us] [does] тупой трюк для [them].
[us] [eyes] [them] and [scoffs], "Решил, что сегодня день без штанов?"
[us] [makes] отвращенное лицо [them]. "Фу, ты грязнуля!"
[us] [exclaims] взволнованно.
[us] медленно [claps] покачивая [us-pos] головой, затем [turns] и уходит.
[us] [recites] некоторую терминологию [them], демонстрируя [us-pos] технические знания.
[us] [nods] и [listens] внимательно.
[us-pos] глаза немного затуманиваются.
[us] [tells] [them] [us-subj] не вдаваться в подробности. Кому это интересно?
Не желая оставаться в долгу, [us] [fires] еще более эзотерическим техническим жаргоном.
[us] [starts] рассказывать [them] старые военные истории.
[us] [is] странную, захватывающую историю, и [us-subj] [tells] ее подробно.
[us] [rolls] [us-pos] глаза и вздыхает.
[us] [banters] with [them] о недавнем матче Breakout.
[us] [re-enacts] игру из последнего матча, бросая воображаемый мяч [them].
[us] и [them] вспоминают, как Shooters разгромили Warriors.'
[us] [gloats] над [them] рассказывая, как Warriors уничтожили [them-pos] драгоценных Shooters.
[us] [catches] мяч. Наклоняясь и уклоняясь, [us-subj] [beats] трех защитников и [shoots] по воротам.
[us] [admits], что [us-subj] давно не смотрел Breakout.
[us] эффектно [fumbles] мяч. [them] [boos].

[us] [isn't] интересно.

[us] [gasps] от шока.

[us] [smacks] [them] немного.
[us] [smacks] [them] немного.

[us] [curses], и [makes] жест, чтобы отвратить несчастье.
[us] [scoffs], что все это ерунда.

strTitle: Назвать своего контакта в правоохранительных органах
strDesc: [us] [says] что Офицер [3rd] [has] о некоторых сделках [them-pos]. Но, учитывая, что офицер [3rd] [is] другом, [us] возможно сможем сбить [3rd-pos] с толку. При условии, что [them] [is] вести себя хорошо.

[us] [insults] [them] жестоко.

[us] [subtracts] one month salary from death pay.

strTitle: Tell them to Fuck Off Forever
strDesc: [us] [tells] [them] to fuck off. Forever.

[us] [tells] [them] to take this job and shove it. Nobody's gonna tell [us-obj] what to do anymore.
[us] [says] nobody respects [us-obj] on this ship.
[us] [says] the job is too dangerous. [us-contractIs] out.

strTitle: Ask Them to Stay on the Crew
strDesc: [us] [asks] [them] to remain a member of the crew based on new terms.
[us] [says] [them] should stick around if [them-subj] [knows] what's good for [them-obj]. Space is a dangerous place for people without friends.
[us] [says] [them] [has] been very impressive lately and [us-offers] some new, vital jobs onboad if [them-subj] [agrees] to stay.

strTitle: Add $1000 to a One Time Bonus
strDesc: [us] [adds] $1000 to a one time bonus.
[us] [adds] $25 per day to [them-pos] current salary.
[us] [adds] one month salary to [them-pos] death pay.
[us] [describes] a few of the harsh economic realities in the system and [suggests] that [them] [doesn't] have the skills to find a better position than the one [them-subj] [has] now.
[us] [says] [us-subj] would rather work with a crew that values [us-pos] input and contributions. [us-subj] [quits], and the decision is final.
[us] [says] [us-subj] [doesn't] want to spend [us-pos] last moments on this ship. [us-subj] [quits], and the decision is final.

[us] [quits].
[us] says [us-subj] [appreciates] having a more integral role onboard. [us-subj] [accepts] the new terms and agree to stay.
After calming down a bit, [us] [decides] to stay onboard for now.

strTitle: Remain on Crew out of Fear
strDesc: [us] [shrinks] away and [agrees] that [them] probably [knows] what is best. [them-subj] will remain a member of the crew for now.
[us] [tells] [them] to double fuck off.
[us] [is] grateful for [us-pos] time with [them].
[us] [asks] the officers if there is a problem.
[us] [asks] [them] if [them-subj] [knows] why they have been boarded.
[us] [opens] the UI.
[us] [offers] a warm greeting and [asks] [them] how [them-subj] [has] been since they last spoke.
[us] [says] [us-subj] can't complain and [asks] how long [them] [has] been flying around this bucket of bolts.
[us] [asks] [them] what spacers have been talking about in the criminal underworld.
[us] [says] [us-subj] are beginning to think that the Black Market fixer who hangs out in The Mescaform is actually on the Ayotimiwa payroll.
[us] [says] [us-subj] heard that a bunch of roughnecks are slapping a dozen thrusters on old salvage pods and doing circuit races around an asteroid field twenty klicks out from K-Leg.

[us] [says] [us-subj] heard kids are trying to summon The Mariner on purpose.
They're putting on purple jumpsuits and smoking like two ounces of Damask Rose.
Now half the abandoned shops in the Old Emporium smell like bongwater and BO.
[us] [asks] [them] if the suits at Ayotimiwa are up to their old tricks.

[us] [says] that Harbourmaster Adeyemi just built a new wing on his place in the NoCo Villas.
It has a private port in the case the Big Man needs a quick exit.
He's growing more paranoid by the day. Thinks every slamming door is the inaugural gunshot of La Revolucion.

[us] [says] the more puritanical Ayo execs are squawking about shuttering the Old Emporium again.
Something about productivity loss.
What those humps don't realize is the Mescaform is the only reason spacers on this rock don't kill eachother.
Not too often, anyway.

[us] [says] the fines for illegal salvage are scheduled to go up again next year.
Ayo loves the resale business, but you better believe they are going to find out exactly how much they can squeeze you without making you stop.
[us] [asks] [them] if [them-subj] [has] confiscated anything good lately.

[us] [says] some headcase on the shipbreaker floor killed his boss with the first tool he could find. Guy confessed immediately so the murder weapon is of no evidentiary value. [them] can have it if they want.
[us] [says] why not? Guy clearly doesn't need it anymore.
[us] [hands] over the tool, [tells] [them-obj] not to do anything too stupid with it, and [us-heads] back to [us-pos] patrol ship.

[us] [says] thanks but [us-subj] [doesn't] need a tool that badly.
[them] [shrugs], then [departs] for their patrol vehicle.

[us] [says] [us-subj] pulled security detail for Ayo exec party up in NoCo and it was quite the soiree.
The host gave them a case of leftover booze as a parting gift.
Want it?
[us] [says] hell yeah. Love drinking on the boss's dime.
[us] [hands] over the booze and [tells] [them] to take it easy.

[us] [says] thanks but [us-subj] [doesn't] need that much booze on a ship.
[them] [wishes] [us-obj] good luck and [heads] back to [them-pos] patrol ship.
[us] [flashes] a maniacal grin and [tells] [them] this one won't go down easy.

strTitle: Ask for Their Badge and Credentials
strDesc: [us] [asks] to see [them-pos] badge and security license.
[us] [attempts] to change the mood of [them] via another angle.
[us] [attempts] to change the mood of [them] via another angle.
[us] [tells] [them] that [us-pos] uniform is [us-pos] credential.
[us] [says] to take a step back.
[us] [flashes] [them] a legitimate seeming badge.
[us] [promises] [us-subj] will write an incident report of the docking upon return to [us-pos] cruiser.
[us] quickly [returns] the badge to [us-pos] inner pocket.
[us] [thanks] the officer for being so diligent and [suggests] that AyoSec is the only thing keeping the OKLG system from falling into chaos.
[us] [agrees] wholeheartedly and [says] [us-subj] [appreciates] meeting someone who understands the role of the badge. [us-subj] [lets] [them] off with a warning and [wishes] them a pleasant day.
[us] [sees] through [them-pos] shameless ass-kissing, and continues with [us-pos] protocol.
[us] [says] [us-subj] [needs] a minute to think.

strTitle: Admit to Having No Active Transponder
strDesc: [them] [says] [us] [knows] that [us-subj] [was] flying dark.
[us] [says] it is illegal and highly dangerous to fly without an actively broadcasting transponder.

strTitle: Make Up a Plausible Excuse for Flying Dark
strDesc: [us] [invents] a reason for flying dark that would be reasonable if it were true.
[us] [accepts] the excuse and [tells] [them] to be more careful in the future.
[us] [doesn't] buy the excuse and instead [renders] [them] a fine for flying dark.

strTitle: Make a Joke about Flying Dark
strDesc: [us] [makes] light of the fact that [us-subj] [is] flying dark, and even [makes] a little joke about the situation.
[us] [laughs] and [banters] back before letting [them] off with a warning.
[us] [says] flying dark is nothing to joke about and [renders] [them] a significant fine.
[us] [says] [them] [is] flying a ship that has been reported stolen. The officer brings [them] into AyoSec custody.
[us] [says] that [us-subj] [has] [us-pos] Ogiso's work safety poster prominently displayed in a high traffic area as per local regulations.

strTitle: Confirm They are Doing Random Ogiso Inspection
strDesc: [us] [confirms] [us-subj] [is] doing a routine Ogiso's inspection.
[us] [says] it seems like the crew has made a good faith effort to follow regulations and [lets] [them] off with a warning.
[us] [says] the crew has not been following Ogiso's regulations and [issues] a minor fine of $1500 to [them].
[us] [pays] the fine and [asks] [them] what the Ogiso boot tastes like.
[us] [pays] the fine and [says] [us-subj] [is] happy that Ogiso has their back.
[us] [says] this is more serious than some petty regulation
[us] [tries] to bait [them] into an easy win over a small infraction.

strTitle: Admit that the Ship Is Not Yet Spaceworthy
strDesc: [us] [says] that the ship is a bit of a work in progress
[us] [confirms] that the hull does not pass basic inspection and [issues] a minor fine of $1000 to [them].
[us] [pays] the fine and [insists] that while an ugly ship can be repaired the same can't be said for [them-pos] face.
[us] [pays] the fine and [thanks] [them] for their service.
[us] [says] [us-subj] [doesn't] care how busted the ship is.
[us] [admits] that [us-subj] docked in order to salvage the derelict.

strTitle: Confirm That Salvage Operation is Illegal

[us] [takes] the license from [them], [us-examines] it a minute, then [us-hands] it back.
Making a note of the exchange, [us] [thanks] [them] for [them-pos] time, and [us-turns] to leave.
[us] [pays] the fine while telling the officers exactly what animals they resemble.
[us] [pays] the fine, and [tells] the officers to fly safe.
[us] [says] [us-subj] [doesn't] care what scrappers do with junk in the boneyard.
[us] [says] [us-subj] [has] no idea why [us-contractHas] been boarded.
"Wait a minute," [us] [says], looking at [them].
"Mmm-hmm," [us] [exhales], unimpressed.
[us] [looks] pensive, like [us-subj] [is] considering something.

[us] [says] [them] was randomly selected for boarding investigation, but all appears to be in order here.
Making a note of the incident, [us] [thanks] [them] for [them-pos] time, and [us-turns] to leave.
[us] [suggests] [us-subj] may have some previously unpaid fines [us-subj] [has] been meaning to settle...

[us] [scoffs] at [them-pos] offer and [us-demands] [them-subj] hand over what [them-subj] [is] carrying.
[us] [adds] the bribe to [us-pos] ledger and [wishes] [them] a pleasant day.
[us] [grins], [adds] the bribe to [us-pos] ledger, and [gives] [them] [us-pos] contact info in case [them-subj] ever [needs] a 'friend' in the near future.
[us] [tells] [them] [us-subj] [is] worth more than that.
[us] [declines] the bribe and [demands] that [them] [shows] more respect for the badge.
[us] [declines] the bribe and [insists] that while it may not feel like it all the time around here, some people take their job seriously.
[us] [appears] annoyed that the situation is out of [us-pos] control. [us-subj] [scowls] at [them], fuming in silence.
[us] [appears] on-edge, and [demands] that [them] not make any sudden moves.
[us] [reaches] [us-pos] boiling point and [lashes] out at [them] with a brutal reprimand.
[us] [has] completely lost [us-pos] patience and [says] [us-subj] [isn't] even going to waste any more time finding a legitimate infraction. [us] [assigns] [them] a hefty, bullshit fine.
[us] [pays] the fine while telling the officers exactly what animals they resemble.
[us] [pays] the fine and [tells] the officers to fly safe.
[us] [says] that [them] should take AyoSec officers more seriously and [demands] that [them-subj] [hands] over what [them-subj] [is] carrying.
[us] [asks] [them] to tell [us-obj] about [them-pos] home.
[us] [tells] [them] [us-subj] would rather not talk about home.
[us] [tells] [them] that [us-subj] [is] from Earth.

strTitle: Ask What Life is Like on Earth
strDesc: [us] [asks] [them] what life is like on Earth.

strTitle: Describe the Best Parts of Earth
strDesc: [us] [says] that it was as beautiful as everyone says. You could walk for fifty miles in a forest and still find more trees.

strTitle: Describe the Worst Parts of Earth
strDesc: [us] [says] that it was a rotten planet even before the ablation cascade cut it off from the rest of the system.

strTitle: Share a Childhood Memory from Earth
strDesc: [us] [remembers] a lake that would freeze in the winter and melt in the summer. There was a rope swing on the lake and a big rock you could climb and then leap from.
[us] [tells] [them] that [us-subj] [is] from Mars.

strTitle: Ask What Life is Like on Mars
strDesc: [us] [asks] [them] what life is like on Mars.
[us] [says] that [us-subj] grew up on Hangzhou Orbital, which is a paradise for those who can afford it. [us-subj] [hopes] to one day have enough money to return there and buy a residential visa.

strTitle: Describe the Worst Parts of Mars
strDesc: [us] [says] that Tharsis Landing is riven by factional violence. Residents of The Labyrinth are constantly clashing with Martian Security, or else taking their bribes.

[us] [recalls] thousands of low single story homes surrounded green siheyuan courtyards. [us] would ride pedicabs down through the hutong because [us-pos] parents wouldn't let [us-obj] ride the metro.
[us] [tells] [them] that [us-subj] [is] from Venus.

strTitle: Ask What Life is Like on Venus
strDesc: [us] [asks] [them] what life is like on Venus.

[us] [says] that life on a Venusian aerostat isn't so different than life on a big station. But Newcal is getting very crowded and [us] [is] excited for the upcoming launch of the aerostat Los Angeles.

strTitle: Describe the Worst Parts of Venus
strDesc: [us] [says] that the piracy around the Newcal aerostat colony is getting worse every day. If the crew refuses to cooperate, the pirates will force the entire ship down to the crushing atmosphere at Venus's surface.

[us] [says] [us-subj] grew up watching the RCS drag races outside the Brazilian aerostat colony 'Cloudbreak.' The little coffins cut through the dense yellow sulfuric fog of the Venusian atmosphere like it wasn't even there.

strTitle: Say You Are from Jupiter System
strDesc: [us] [tells] [them] that [us-subj] grew up near Jupiter.

strTitle: Ask What Region They are From
strDesc: [us] [asks] [them] what part of the Jovian system [them-subj] [is] from.

strTitle: Talk About the Best Parts of Europa
strDesc: [us] [says] that they grew up in Atlantis, a submarine colony beneath the frozen oceans of Europa. [us] [misses] Europa, whose population mostly ignores the messy political and corporate drama of the inner planets.

strTitle: Talk About the Worst parts of Europa
strDesc: [us] [says] that they grew up in Atlantis, a submarine colony beneath the frozen oceans of Europa. [us] [insists] the cliffside colony was damp and miserable and full of researchers who were intentionally obtuse.

[us] [says] that they grew up in Atlantis, a submarine colony beneath the frozen oceans of Europa. [us] [recalls] a childhood trip to Point Mosman and the tendrilled iridescent Lusca drifting in and out of the thermal vents.

strTitle: Describe the Best Parts of Ganymede
strDesc: [us] [says] that they grew up on Ganymede, the only truly free colony left in the system. [us] [misses] the solidarity of building a liberated society among laboring Confederates.

strTitle: Describe the Worst Parts of Ganymede
strDesc: [us] [says] that they grew up on Ganymede, the only truly 'free' colony left in the system. [us] [complains] that it is impossible to get ahead there because the Confederacy won't let anyone start a private mining claim.

[us] [says] that they grew up on Ganymede, the only truly free colony left in the system. [us] [recalls] staring into the sky during the Coup and watching the Guns of Ganymede flash teal and pink as they obliterated a fleet of decelerating corporate mercenary frigates.
